rulings.Case Title: T&C PROPERTIES, LLC VS. ST. JAMES PROPERTY CO., LLC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioner Respondent Tandc Properties Llcs Second Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Award Of Attorneys Fees And Costs In The Post Arbitration Judicial Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 10, 2023 line 1. T & C PROPERTIES, LLC'S SECOND AMENDED MOTION FOR AN AWARD OF ATTORNEY'S FEES AND COSTS IN THE POST ARBITRATION JUDICIAL PROCEEDINGS is DENIED. T & C is not the prevailing party in the litigation phase of the dispute. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: T&C PROPERTIES, LLC VS. ST. JAMES PROPERTY CO., LLC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Award Of Attorneys Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 10, 2023 line 1. ST. JAMES PROPERTY CO., LLC'S MOTION FOR AWARD OF ATTORNEY'S FEES AND COSTS is GRANTED in part. Hearing Required in part. Motion is GRANTED as to the fees incurred in the litigation phase of the dispute. St. James is the prevailing party. No later than 9:00 a.m. on August 10, 2023 Respondent to provide a supplemental declaration stating the final amount of the fees incurred in the litigation phase AND a copy of the previously filed declarations with the portions of the documents evidencing the amounts claimed (e.g. bills, invoices, summaries) tabbed and highlighted. Hearing Required to address the fees incurred in arbitration. The Court is inclined to send the issue of the amounts of the fees awarded, if any, in the arbitration phase back to arbitration. The Final Award stops just short of calculating the amount after determining the prevailing party and its entitlement to the fees and costs. However, if the parties stipulate to the Court's authority to determine the issue of amount of fees, if any, the Court will consider making a ruling on the merits to avoid any prejudice to the parties caused by further delay in final resolution of this dispute submitted to arbitration in 2019. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
